entries.title: Socratic Method
entries.content: Teaching through questioning to expose assumptions and stimulate critical thinking. Named after Socrates of ancient Athens.

entries.title: Ethics: Utilitarianism
entries.content: Actions are right if they maximize overall happiness or well-being (greatest good for greatest number). Associated with Bentham and Mill.

entries.title: Cogito Ergo Sum
entries.content: Descartes: 'I think, therefore I am.' Foundational certainty in philosophy—existence proved by thinking.

entries.title: Logical Fallacies
entries.content: Errors in reasoning: ad hominem (attack person), straw man (distort argument), false dilemma (only two options), appeal to authority.

entries.title: Plato's Allegory of the Cave
entries.content: Prisoners see only shadows; leaving cave means discovering true reality. Metaphor for education and philosophical enlightenment.
